prompt:
Goal: editorial illustration for a programming textbook
Scene: The version number "2.4.1" displayed large in the center, with the three segments pulled apart and color-coded with connecting dots between them. The "2" is red with a warning/breaking icon (explosion) and labeled "MAJOR - breaking changes". The "4" is blue with a gift/feature icon (wrapped present) and labeled "MINOR - new features". The "1" is green with a bandage/fix icon (adhesive bandage) and labeled "PATCH - bug fixes". Below, three example version bumps are shown: breaking change increments major, new feature increments minor, bug fix increments patch. The overall look is like a medical chart or anatomy diagram.
Style: Head First book illustration style, clean lines, slightly whimsical and humorous, warm colors, educational
Aspect ratio: 16:9
Background: white
Text in image: minimal or none
Avoid: photorealistic, dark, scary, complex UI screenshots
